title: WinForms Button click handler crashes with null-valued expression RuntimeException
description: System.Management.Automation.RuntimeException 'You cannot call a method on a null-valued expression' thrown inside a WinForms Button.OnClick event handler. The compiled lambda (lambda_method1396) called a method or accessed a property on a variable that was $null at the time of the button click. Root cause: either a control variable went out of scope, a lookup returned $null without a guard, or a $Script: variable was never initialised before the handler fired.
exception_message: System.Management.Automation.RuntimeException: You cannot call a method on a null-valued expression.
affected_file: Unknown — stack frame lambda_method1396 is a compiled event handler; exact file/line requires adding Write-AppLog to the handler try/catch
affected_line: Unknown — lambda frame has no PS source line
fix_notes: 1. Locate all Button.Add_Click scriptblocks in the codebase. 2. Add try/catch with Write-AppLog to each. 3. Identify which variable is $null at click time (add $null -ne guards). 4. Reference SIN-PATTERN-022 remedy for the fix pattern.

attempts.notes: Finding no longer detected at original location. Resolved externally.
